title: ICU restraint documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports objective multidisciplinary risk assessment, prior incident and trigger context, service-user involvement, previously effective de-escalation and transfer of an existing risk assessment.
red_flags.rationale: Supports risk assessment and de-escalation before restrictive intervention for ICU restraint documentation; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports objective multidisciplinary risk assessment, prior incident and trigger context, service-user involvement, previously effective de-escalation and transfer of an existing risk assessment.

source.title: Violence and aggression: short-term management in mental health, health and community settings

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.8–1.2.14 and 1.3.12–1.3.20 — risk assessment and de-escalation

examination.text: Supports documenting airway, breathing, circulation, vital signs, physical and psychological monitoring and clinician review only when a restrictive intervention actually occurred. Medicine instructions and doses are excluded.
examination.rationale: Supports safety monitoring during restrictive intervention for ICU restraint documentation; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting airway, breathing, circulation, vital signs, physical and psychological monitoring and clinician review only when a restrictive intervention actually occurred. Medicine instructions and doses are excluded.

source.title: Violence and aggression: short-term management in mental health, health and community settings

follow_up.text: Supports recording harm, ongoing risk, emotional impact, contributing factors, patient and witness perspectives, completed documentation, handover and formal review after an incident.
follow_up.rationale: Supports post-incident review and documentation for ICU restraint documentation; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports recording harm, ongoing risk, emotional impact, contributing factors, patient and witness perspectives, completed documentation, handover and formal review after an incident.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Behaviour, immediate risk, alternatives, capacity, authorization, restraint type, checks, injury, duration and review are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, escalation or disposition is generated for ICU restraint documentation.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
